textByHashes.92ca4aa6ad6ee2df54a39b513f7e33c50a47d127: Ah, hello and welcome. I'm Martin Holt, leader of Kourend's Royal Society. This is our little protest against the Kourend Council.
textByHashes.79d213bae8dda0d947bd1f5c734684f1d218b4ee: Now there's a question! I have a better one. What's right with the Council?
textByHashes.584d879c13610f71cb03887cab0b4dbc00c9ac9a: I bet you didn't know that the Council was originally only temporary. After the last King died, they were only supposed to rule until his daughter came of age.
textByHashes.b1a3d67381559bf5c94ac8400f73aaf017b64531: However, according to the Council, the King's daughter went mad. Such a wonderful convenience for them of course. With her out of the picture, the Council became permanent.
textByHashes.6f067c14125d182ef0ea7c79404f532bd61bc770: Democracy! That's meant to be the big thing for the Council. Funny thing is, although they're meant to be elected by the people, the same four people have won every single election. Little odd, no?
textByHashes.762c986b2a60b82e592ca6caa1b2da4633dc905d: Indeed it is. So, do you want to join our protest?
textByHashes.bb9eafe5b6869ef605da4728263a2e284b118160: Oh my friend, suspicious is the dish of the day whenever the Council is around. And what I've just told you barely scratches the surface. Things have been getting even worse recently.
textByHashes.02468fcef25247812d1d3e3d45b0755fa908b75c: Do you know of Councillor Sophia Hughes? Lovely lady. Spends her free time with the Disciples of Yama, a wonderful cult of Zamorakian demon summoners.
textByHashes.e137168f06d4c6b60565eb45c3fb41aeda48d265: Ah, so you're the adventurer who's been cooking up a storm around here. Well you'll be pleased to know your hard work was all for nothing. Councillor Hughes has escaped. Lucky her.
textByHashes.bdbc49871ff142c31be1bce8bb8a274fafbcb744: Believe it or not, no one actually knows. The Council is trying to keep it all on the down low of course. Almost like they have something to hide.
textByHashes.b92b275c5c8573db3b373bc73bee78e0a378fb69: Oh, we will soon! Just you give it time. They'll slip up sooner or later.
textByHashes.57bc5c1449608245b1b569bb92ad725687477c18: What an interesting idea. I suppose you would be well placed to have a little nosey, considering your history with the Councillor. Though you'd need to get permission, of course.
textByHashes.59b7ba68ad13653f8c3277a29d230e5893125c3d: I hear the Kourend Royal Guard are leading the investigation. Commander Lydia Fullore is their leader. You'll find her in the castle.
textByHashes.e2c926863e2ffe5b2b34e9ba5e1ba0d4c66f1d3d: Good luck friend. Your eyes are open now. Don't trust the Council.
textByHashes.5d00615f7d12f2e4a25d11518ac346cefc848e25: As you wish, but you shouldn't be afraid to open your eyes. If you change your mind, we'll be here.
textByHashes.4b16c745f1278298f445e43959ced18a9a613c18: She'll be somewhere in the castle.
textByHashes.693d0c8e17d9351a8bec5657ee3e019e6f7edf91: Hello again. I hope things are going well.
textByHashes.55fc0f746ae6ad4cf9b7a82627f9c20f39513e41: Well, keep up the good work. After all, it would be very awkward for our friends on the Council if you caught Sophia Hughes again.
textByHashes.e3b67a66ffd0524a99b6b5afb05e0bd4a3bb9d3c: Hello again, friend. Isn't it a wonderful day? Sophia Hughes has been dealt with and our wonderfully corrupt Council are out rowing without a paddle. Keep up the good work!
textByHashes.24c256f7b423e02f73f92d4699d0dd2b9b4d2ccf: Good to see you, friend. Excellent work with Sophia Hughes!
textByHashes.820602c3701624f20835e6188f0cc0690ab79087: Indeed it is, friend. Would you believe it that apparently some councillor went and summoned a demon in Port Piscarilius? That made a lot of people angry.
textByHashes.c7f92fcbe31cab497693c503e35792f0e9b3764f: No doubt sent by the Council to keep an eye on us. We've got them scared now.
textByHashes.4d45491a1210823f5e7428a2b4485d87ee8fc5ff: And yet they're the ones with the weapons. Interesting, huh?
textByHashes.b001c31e4888f8fc5f9ea2d72b4652c6b2e211d6: Anyway, what can I do for you, friend?
textByHashes.13af4368e019c40b24ad6a0d324186e575ed4ff1: Something important? Is it about the Council?
textByHashes.db553981b21650a2751424e7d5f570980b0bdeca: Well, what is it? If you have news on the Council, we must let the people know at once.
textByHashes.7cc7ba221cb1a2f5916c692fd463c8e11f6b30b1: The people have a right to know, my friend.
textByHashes.64791818710a82131f0d3c22527ef41646547fa5: I see. This sounds serious. What have you learnt?
textByHashes.89e54e8fe6dd90fac54ad0cfce6d21d0d84231f5: A man and his daughter? Surely this can't mean...
textByHashes.73bcd6627f56487e12c5897f38b252fc1aef367f: I knew it! I knew the Council were involved in his death!
textByHashes.69bce0d08f775212082ddae284a62cb4163675e5: And this is why you came to me? You have a plan?
textByHashes.e436afdf4d0398a28c1c0b4f14a4ff544d79571b: But my friend, no one knows where Rose is! Some don't even think she's alive any more. Sadly, most people have just forgotten her completely.
textByHashes.148f70db2d9c15e0359eb31f45ec24c78e107fab: I'm afraid not. However, I wonder if Rose herself might be able to help.
textByHashes.5c2473cd55e341a5fa004660dec41832c6b13f2f: The Council didn't declare Rose mad right away. It wasn't until a few weeks after her father died that she was moved away.
textByHashes.8f53d244ab44398a02d54b0bf0d8d9a9d3aede8a: Rose was well known for being a good writer, even at her young age. She documented everything she did. She even wrote her father's memoirs for him.
textByHashes.85ced1f9514e360241ec3d7ce8b5f7d04e49a61c: If something happened in those few weeks, maybe she wrote about it.
textByHashes.9e773c666a7099fcc2601ca7da346af82c928281: You did? Interesting. The memoirs were auctioned off recently, along with a lot of other royal possessions.
textByHashes.cd0635fb69998d9c9d1a0c4a6a670d35f54382f9: The auction was a request from the King before he died. He wanted the money to go towards helping the poor of Kourend.
textByHashes.e8c86b1d7a16380bb8f3df045464d9802ddbd8fe: And you're only mentioning this now? You had proof that something happened to Rose, and you did nothing with it?
textByHashes.8d1ba7603f1577e696caa0b81b791aea762bfcbd: Even still, something like that might have been worth bringing up.
textByHashes.5d48ba60e9aa2b9ad0f2a96d294b82754f126bdf: The memoirs were auctioned off recently, along with a lot of other royal possessions. The auction was a request from the King before he died. He wanted the money to go towards helping the poor of Kourend.
textByHashes.d83a2412934b95c330c5acb65b1875b338d2e2dc: For that you can thank our Council friends. They blocked it for years. They said Rose's things should not be included, as the original request only covered her father's possessions.
textByHashes.981ce78f9b35fb8c90af0fe3bca63c8554a69b42: I did wonder if they had more malicious motivations, and now you've confirmed it. They clearly didn't want anyone getting their hands on Rose's writings.
textByHashes.908e846d213a933e9accae41e7df28c368a1d408: Yes. Rose's personal diary. It went to the Library Historical Archive. I hear they have it on display there.
textByHashes.e893300bf423cbfbce9ff78f0bbfa0d4a32276df: The Archive isn't the Library. You can't just borrow things. I doubt they'll let you take it.
textByHashes.f9b9a1f6ced0c05d97041af85064626f2ff04fc9: A man who knows what needs to be done. I respect that. Talk to Archeio in the Arceuus Library. He can take you to the Archive. Come back once you have the diary and we'll discuss our next steps.
textByHashes.ba5582c4982e3a2f9e2da81f2c81b0d9f32beced: A woman who knows what needs to be done. I respect that. Talk to Archeio in the Arceuus Library. He can take you to the Archive. Come back once you have the diary and we'll discuss our next steps.
textByHashes.645c634abb6d27b92d247d6fdd39c2d4f406d26e: Rose's personal diary is on display in the Library Historical Archive. We need to aquire it. Talk to Archeio in the Arceuus Library. He can take you to the Archive.
textByHashes.b47fdf4dc6fa7def0bbe04a775943ffdfc7fde0b: Good to see you, friend. Any luck with that diary?
textByHashes.e17dbec086b82c7af7ebb05798987a1a685f0e48: Good work. Now, let's see if this diary has anything for us...
textByHashes.b0c5ce402f2e9c350078c7f96caffd00f964c66a: Hmm. Doesn't look to be anything obvious, but that's not suprising. Anything too in your face would have been found by the Council long ago.
textByHashes.e65b8d4d4da764bfe3ca3134b7a71364bdd15777: Let's see if the same trick has been used here.
textByHashes.0b98422c32103af763d206657348311fb454c405: And what do we have here?
textByHashes.0a81e59655996707757b2e4f48365918fa0ccb00: Very interesting. See what you make of this.
textByHashes.df86175c782c18d8a0224dfc9481a86329051c4c: That it is. It seems to be describing a location. If we find that location, maybe we'll find something of use.
textByHashes.94b6f80b8741cc6f6d1e70b5bf3a3ac51a737143: We should split up. The Council could be watching us, and I don't want to make anything too obvious. Let's see who can solve this thing first.
textByHashes.95b597f17af2aca1d885d1d242b4d8a9cf956406: We should split up and try to solve the riddle. I'll regroup with you at wherever it leads us.
textByHashes.3130f1e8ae82d1958722d9e8c6241670d30627e0: Forthos was the seat of the Hosidius family before it was destroyed in the War of the Five Houses. 'A seat of power, lost in a war of pride.'
textByHashes.a2a324a2900dd06840d0c1e107633286110a3187: Anyway, looks like I won this one. What's my prize?
textByHashes.3ff0a5d9afce420064c395270b73f9622076e604: Pah, that's where you're wrong my friend. This could lead us to the ultimate prize. This could lead us to the truth behind the delightful Kourend Council.
textByHashes.3de65525642c52ccea12f542cb528f8cfc793089: I opened my eyes. When I worked for the Council, I was young and naive. But over time, I came to see the truth.
textByHashes.cb818862ddc9c57c76da651d76c114553b0db072: The tipping point came a couple of years ago. I was helping to manage the treasury at the time. We had a big donation come in from a private businessman. Lovely chap.
textByHashes.460ffe3923dbc20a0e472042c9b2afc99e1b7082: He was ever so noble. Something eating away at his conscience, no doubt. He wanted the money to go towards improved education in Port Piscarilius. He felt it would help with the crime levels there.
textByHashes.5d69ba545f2ad034d41096aa39594a968e9c0f5f: That was of course a big no-go for the Council. Helping the people? No chance! Instead, they used only a small amount for that. They spent the rest on castle renovations.
textByHashes.7ae6edf52e73c58d9934ab053bb4ee0f51170cf8: They claimed the increase in castle tourism would make more money in the long run. A load of tosh! I knew it was just for them.
textByHashes.0033937f6636fd4c5e7da18f56df1ac78e2ad8c6: I walked out that same day. Soon after, I founded Kourend's Royal Society. We've been a bane for the Council ever since.
textByHashes.8dcea5bad22b41c53591eb1ecbb5ba680560e6d4: Wasn't really important. I'm sure Lydia didn't tell you everything about herself either. We were together once, you know. But she was ever loyal to the Council.
textByHashes.1293ca23bdc100a710d03a08651ee685e23026e2: In the end she decided I wasn't her type, and went off with that Captain Ginea instead. Of course, that didn't work out either. One day she'll stop blaming her problems on everyone else.
textByHashes.bba51ed6183dbb6b2052be8170a04d522f497a9d: Nope. And neither is my history with the Council. Now, let's crack on. Rose led us here for a reason. There must be something hidden nearby.
textByHashes.46bd69b48469b5ae79778f6d679bbb1a25f400e1: She was born to King Kharedst IV as his first and only child. Her mother died when she was very young, so her father mostly looked after her on his own. The two were very close.
textByHashes.f0cc3e878be8abf1852522086b96158804c7f262: She was only 11 when her father died. Even at that age, she had already proven that she was smart, capable and caring. She'd have made a good queen, but the Council put a stop to that.
textByHashes.5f66c1bb17443042616b83fceb97ecea1ea96b77: You'd hope that even the Council wouldn't go as far as to kill her, but that's probably wishful thinking. They're as evil as they come.
textByHashes.e22ff50248ea6a4b1a5d8cc1dfb792d854f0c5c3: Hopefully she can give us the truth about her father's death.
textByHashes.5368472b799157d7b646f3bae8d473b4839a2aa5: Oh they're guilty my friend, make no mistake. We just need proof. Once we have it, we can use it to have them removed from power.
textByHashes.c5c62c4b29e35705520a522d70e67c9f3ecf75fc: After that, Rose can take her rightful place on the throne.
textByHashes.cc2ee076ecae8e4c3945f022357fe4892f5eeeca: Let's cross that bridge if we get to it.
textByHashes.07bcd73233fd8c5c9ff6a370dbe31c65d85549bf: The throne of Kourend has sat empty for far too long. After all, what is a kingdom without a king? That was the question I asked myself when I created the society.
textByHashes.c2f5d9aeea8607ac991097c32e143a04742d3f27: You see, I'd grown tired of our wonderful Council and their dishonourable ways. I wanted a return to how things used to be. Turns out I wasn't alone.
textByHashes.231df70b1b1fc358296e916d5c741025e8b9dc99: Kourend's Royal Society offers a place for likeminded individuals to share and debate their views without fear of being judged.
textByHashes.4b4972e437960631427da2e949c240ad6dbd0f1a: We know the Council to be corrupt. We feel it only right that all of Kourend know that. The people have a right to the truth.
textByHashes.ba1d647409ee6db9dd7ef03e16589ba32a168bbf: Democracy is just a façade, my friend. The Council! They're meant to be a democracy! It's all a load of rubbish. Democracy is just a way of pretending the people have power.
textByHashes.15b2fc912a088eb33e343fcaa829a32352cdc8ae: Kings and queens are raised from birth to rule. They know how to do it right. Under a king or queen, this kingdom would flourish.
textByHashes.ca6f16462b393e3c98f5545a5f3ecde47c9cbb99: Even with a monarchy, the people still hold the power. Sometimes it just takes them time to realise it.
textByHashes.008aeb5fbeed280b85256c8ece8f72ffe5e5138d: And what happened to him in the end?
textByHashes.16d1e06b551182e8ce10644c01929fafffb500d5: Exactly! Even with a monarchy, the people still hold the power. Sometimes it just takes them time to realise it.
textByHashes.3b71456b8e694492d7e86d32b3787196afd2fa87: I'll have you know I am helping. I'm looking here.
textByHashes.7095eacffd1b7f91e622f64b7cf9e8dbec20d1cb: Another riddle. Well, same again then. Let's split up and see if we can find where it leads.
textByHashes.c49d09a5d1c6bb23d2893024f67cfa18f9189769: We should split up and try to solve the next riddle. I'll regroup with you at wherever it leads us.
textByHashes.a737523e1b2f13b8ad4ebe801d7a3b6bb44e8044: Ah, you made it. Welcome to Kourend's top holiday destination. Here you'll find all sorts of fun things like cold, cold and cold.
textByHashes.b777850a85f854b8db6f86643825c39ade4e7569: Looks like it. Problem is, it's locked and...
textByHashes.85dc48ff56840d261b3cf09cff458227307d718b: Hang on, do you hear that?
textByHashes.c22226be9486276c1a75eefebc976a3f019b7ea1: So now the Council are sending assassins after us. They've got some nerve.
textByHashes.8575083368c7f78b14568f515bb10068c15fce21: Yes, a good point. They clearly don't know what we're doing yet. This must be about the protests instead. They wanted to silence me!
textByHashes.3b145587d50c54e3f854c5ce2389e1cfdd11e4cd: It's the only logical explanation. The Council are running scared. They can feel their grip on Kourend loosening and are desperately trying to stop that.
textByHashes.2e595bef8603df67b4402288b0db7cd9ec535b3f: Very true. I tried to open the panel before you arrived, but it needs a key. Perhaps it's hidden nearby.
textByHashes.e166e54494c8cffe5d72743281390441e34d037e: Excellent. What does this one say?
textByHashes.7c449635b5818ac48fa385c9fd3d9c91815ab205: Another one. I wonder how many there are.
textByHashes.bcc5fc7cf139ef04fd15c89384211fd9d3ced1c8: Indeed. Let's split up again. Be careful though. Who knows if the Council will send another assassin.
textByHashes.f8272fba3000657e74ce7852bff23952564a2810: We should split up and try to solve the next riddle. I'll regroup with you at wherever it leads us. Be careful though. Who knows if the Council will send another assassin.
textByHashes.f09d331a0db2a8d6c063287382e91fc66fe30291: Now this is more like it! A nice temperature and a good source of beer. Not to mention the fact that the Council wouldn't dare send an assassin somewhere so built up. Even they're not that stupid.
textByHashes.cb94c43f598d1697c8d4ac0943c234b788487653: Apparently Rose and her father would often make riddles for each other. I'm guessing she was quite fond of them.
textByHashes.44a6ae0e6bebcdbbab806eff43e4641b0c6b4bb5: Never underestimate a child, my friend. I remember a gang of thieves a few years back that used them heavily. The kids could get places the adults couldn't. No one ever suspected them either.
textByHashes.daf015cfd80014af05ed879754f85d89dbe1954e: They were such a problem that the Council had to get Lord Shayzien to send some soldiers in to deal with them. Lydia was one of them. This was before she joined the Royal Guard.
textByHashes.f5d9a265972af4ba9ad07a361506d2cc5dd2155f: The Council had them all thrown in prison, even the children. Lydia was distraught. Still, it didn't stop her joining the Royal Guard. She was always blind to the truth.
textByHashes.2c72ccc9b0e7d2fd4d3d322952ed47642ff675fd: True, but at least I realised in the end.
textByHashes.a3679b3205b81cd325305ab86a484eda769419a2: Maybe. Anyway, we have a clue to find. Time to get searching.
textByHashes.c668192402685c7cf9cdc8444b23294e8c5687ec: Tomas Lawry! Backbone of the Kourend Council!
textByHashes.f2a3aa1ded2e4e2f76dd52f39f7f9505b554796c: Of course, it's a shame the Council are totally spineless.
textByHashes.f31486e66367c28c56364cba85447cc463e48f12: I'm here for a drink of course. This is a pub, you know. Lovely place to sit back and relax. You should try it some time.
textByHashes.70d34bc8d733ebc7ee8cc4d6410a39541504a14f: I am telling the truth. Is it illegal to go to the pub now?
textByHashes.8ada58ca53bed9ce880c4799cda0fda091427231: Inciting violence? You must be joking! All I've done is express my valid concerns. There is no law against that!
textByHashes.b350c19b96c82ac453d7cb0289bb538344c44448: I have given the people a voice! You cannot silence us all!
textByHashes.e5548523453bd9338d3d02937096143d8508b8c8: Fine! But the people will not stand for this. I will not be responsible for what happens now!
textByHashes.3425c092978c34d68a5b7f984e3deb8418f5cb60: Oh wonderful! There's free food and a bed! Can you believe it? Luxury compared to anything in Port Piscarilius!
textByHashes.b3591953e9ce4e86ca6f4c5a1cd354d680d8e806: Of course. Right down to business. As you can see, I'm a little indisposed right now. Apparently the right to protest isn't so much of a right after all.
textByHashes.d46b22b3f5de0e3e7df5cac295a84d769d0b9c08: Excellent work my friend! From the moment I met you, I knew you were something special. Now, what information do you need on him?
textByHashes.75d8ebf08d32d72dd652f011512a7a09baa32ad7: If I had information on his corruption, I might not be stood in a cell right now.
textByHashes.59102b83e515d370f1bfc1a86e2d02566df5875f: I see. Well, there's a member of the Royal Society who has close access to Councillor Unkar's dealings. He gives me regular reports, but there hasn't been anything of note yet.
textByHashes.3ecb6db0c8e2f78cb0351afdd73e1ce7f7c58382: Still, things have progressed fast recently. Maybe he's had more luck since my imprisonment. As I'm stuck in here, perhaps you should go on my behalf to our usual meeting.
textByHashes.a5f5a5fddb7fd55ce6217d15ea2b642fe8c4cb3d: In the Shayzien Graveyard of Heroes. Look for a dwarf. He'll speak with you if you say the passphrase: 'Evil only triumphs when the good do nothing.'
textByHashes.6237421720bf21604dbee81ad6a02c664cc22c4f: Good luck friend. Finish this for all of us!
textByHashes.976c10cecebef8d4bacddf624e9738371c2b0f73: Not a problem. Now get out there and finish this!
textByHashes.7e854465cdc67a3659cc7ebd1365eb53a745a359: Can you believe it? The coronation of a king! Truth be told, there were times I wondered if this day would ever come.
textByHashes.cab2630f07fb758f86c73d1aa80c88d96c0848d5: Everything and more, my friend. And to think they even let us vote on it as well! Fantastic!
textByHashes.657776f73c98ce1fe8e9154c8a5d96a2866e7cb7: Indeed. Seems that everyone wants to congratulate him... Or maybe they're just here for the food.
textByHashes.873c8b717ad16801dcaa1dd219d4acecaa4465e6: Either way, this will be an event for the ages.
textByHashes.e022998c170d274a4d71dd8d9effcbc82375cfbb: Most of them. Lord Shayzien was here earlier but he left. To be honest, he didn't look to be enjoying himself.
textByHashes.9b2513e037d031dcada0959b06d2d17c5e6270bd: Yes, I heard there was a bit of drama here and there. Still, that's Kourend for you.
textByHashes.d8e39785027a8f3a1ce0fd608e29f71be69ca39c: Now, I'll let you go and mingle. I'm sure the King himself will want a word with you at some point as well.
textByHashes.9cca5bd556502bae0ab46dde4164a9846c08add1: Indeed. I never thought I'd be back here.
textByHashes.0cb3438be30105c6bc57291bd9742d9f624129f5: We won! The Council are out, and we have what Kourend really needed. We have a king!
textByHashes.ff1d8d84c570de8d14fcc414f5c5baad0d675ab1: Of course, King Artur ended up taking the throne from his father a little faster than anyone expected, so he needs plenty of guidance. That's where I come in!
textByHashes.d6f70c7568a9d4c2697a0fe2a140f6ffc1b9176f: Exactly! When the people voted on Rose's Accord, they voted for the Hosidius family to become the new royal family. From a technical standpoint that means they voted for King Artur.
textByHashes.daab772220d24852d23ac8807cb27887b2c746af: Precisely. My job is to advise King Artur and ensure his actions have a positive impact on the people that he now rules.
textByHashes.9352a73a615291de626d744741277d5c2b59f76f: Not too bad. While he lacks experience, the King has a good heart. He wants to do the right thing, and he has the support needed to do it.
textByHashes.5e0de89ee84ceec0718969a81d9a67df6c4ec4aa: Mind you, he could do with listening to his mother less. She's always whispering things in his ear. She forgets that he's the one in charge, not her.
textByHashes.cf0f909869ba3afc0becafebcfde894eecae83b6: Still, I'm sure he just needs time to adjust. I have no doubt that under King Artur, Kourend will prosper like never before.
textByHashes.10986c888a40b69e8075b1f4cdb5c92846cbae17: Only a king can right the wrongs of the Council! And only you can make that happen!
textByHashes.0ffb867b22720102b80a36f6ff7cd845c28511f9: How long will you keep your eyes closed? Their corruption surrounds you!
textByHashes.e4b8bfc024356fc8a392dd5c27d4a6013ef81625: This kingdom will never be safe while the Council rule!
textByHashes.49619b3dc4204e52b3b5cf793b0243fed8abd78d: You deserve the truth! They won't give it to you!
textByHashes.26fd0cda780f4a1e3ba30fa998233625ed3bc459: The Council have wronged you! It's time to make things right!
textByHashes.f2b7a5a9245f1a49e82490cae3239cd32a8e0993: Together, we can bring an end to the treachery of the Council!
textByHashes.07945e2f6941240d4cc956baa34e266e8b35becf: The Council care only for themselves! It's time for a ruler who cares for you!
textByHashes.3fe8c3c03f435c0ebb8ab710df6f7529ef09110e: Kourend is a kingdom! A kingdom needs a king, not a council!
textByHashes.4c96a1b5b918a131d6a34d0ef26c050ee5aa878e: Democracy? What democracy? It's all a façade!
textByHashes.9f0a77983483bfe522af7fbd52010c910cff341d: People of Kourend, you have been lied to!
